mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month momentum in INDPRO signals improving real output and operating leverage, raising expected earnings revisions and producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month INDPRO growth is followed by lower 20-day S&P 500 returns with Rank IC below 0 over a 3-year rolling walk-forward window.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses more than 0.3 percentage point above its 12-month trailing low, the labor market has entered a recession-warning phase that lowers expected earnings and produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses above its 12-month trailing minimum by more than 0.3 percentage point
evidence_refs: fact:By 2008-05-15 the unemployment rate had risen from its prior-cycle low
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the conditional mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate combined with negative PAYEMS momentum identifies policy easing caused by economic stress rather than benign discount-rate relief, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS declines over 3 months while PAYEMS declines over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:By 2008-05-15 the Federal Reserve had cut rates sharply while payroll growth had weakened
failure_condition: Reject if the FEDFUNDS-down and PAYEMS-down interaction has nonnegative average 60-day forward returns or no incremental predictive power versus either proxy alone.

mechanism_narrative: A large decline in HOUST signals tightening credit, falling household balance-sheet collateral, and weaker construction demand, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: HOUST is more than 15% below its 12-month moving average and below its level 6 months earlier
evidence_refs: fact:By 2008-05-15 housing starts were far below the mid-2000s expansion levels
failure_condition: Reject if HOUST-collapse triggers are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward cases or if downside hit rate is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL inflation while FEDFUNDS remains elevated tightens real financial conditions for consumers and firms, compressing valuation multiples and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 12-month inflation exceeds 3.5% and FEDFUNDS exceeds 2.5%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint high-inflation and high-FEDFUNDS state does not underperform the unconditional 20-day S&P 500 return by at least 25 basis points on average.

mechanism_narrative: Positive 3-month growth in RSAFS signals resilient nominal consumption demand and near-term revenue support for listed firms, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Reject if higher 3-month RSAFS growth has a negative 5-day forward return slope or Rank IC below 0 across two consecutive 3-year walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: PAYEMS contraction with a simultaneous UNRATE rise confirms that income growth and hiring demand are weakening together, reducing expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS falls over 3 months and UNRATE rises at least 0.2 percentage point over 3 months
failure_condition: Reject if the PAYEMS-down and UNRATE-up interaction has positive mean 20-day forward returns or if its hit rate for negative returns is below 50%.

mechanism_narrative: Weak GDPC1 growth combined with declining INDPRO identifies a broad real-demand slowdown that lowers aggregate sales and margins, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth is below 0.5% annualized and INDPRO is below its level 3 months earlier
failure_condition: Reject if triggered broad-slowdown observations fail to underperform the unconditional 60-day S&P 500 return or if the interaction coefficient is not negative in walk-forward tests.
